title: Lecture Three Domain-Wall Edge-Mode Dependency Graph
summary: Dependency snapshot for the Lecture Three flagship proof: the half-level anomaly and its cancellation set up the doubled system, the folding step produces the domain-wall equation, the wall ansatz yields the chiral boundary equation, and normalizability plus bulk interpretation complete the theorem.
assumptions: The proof order matters because the wall-mode synthesis depends on the doubled starting point and the folding step.
regime: Proof-order audit for the edge-mode theorem family.
scope: Ordered prerequisite spine for the Lecture Three theorem.

source_anchors.notes: Entire proof order sits in this lecture-three section.

representation: Dependency snapshot for the Lecture Three proof order.
derivation_spine: Derive the half-level anomaly of one massive Dirac fermion.
Cancel that anomaly by doubling with opposite masses before introducing a boundary sign flip.
Fold the boundary problem into a sign-changing-mass Dirac equation on all of space.
Insert the localized wall ansatz and derive the chiral boundary equation.
Use the mass sign flip to prove normalizability of the wall mode.
Reconnect the local wall mode to the consistent integer Hall boundary story.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must preserve the exact order of the Lecture Three derivation.
